blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: You want us to alibi each other.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I want us to understand what we can and can't account for. Before someone else does it for us.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: These are our friends, Dean. Marcus just died. And we're sitting here—

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I know how it feels. But if there's any chance this wasn't natural — and Davenport clearly thinks there is — then we need to be ahead of it. Not behind it.
Sophie looked at Wren. Wren gave the smallest nod — barely visible, the kind that said I don't like it either but he's right.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Fine. Yesterday afternoon then. After Marcus went upstairs.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I walked the beach. Alone. I was gone maybe forty minutes, came back empty handed. You all remember I came back without any shells.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: I was inside by then. I didn't see you.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I saw you leave. I didn't watch where you went.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: So nobody can actually confirm where I was for forty minutes.
He said it matter-of-factly. Like he was reporting something about someone else.
